rationale.D1: No `interface *IO` exists in the 2026 repo; ArmSIM and FlywheelSIM extend concrete hardware classes rather than implementing a swappable interface, so there is no IO layer — only vendored CTRE swerve (TunerConstants/CommandSwerveDrivetrain) plus inheritance-based sim subclasses on two mechanisms.
rationale.D2: Superstructure provides coordinated commands fanning out to Arm+Flywheel (stow, ampScore, speakerClose, speakerFar, intakeGround) and is used in AutoRoutines, but there are no state enums, no wanted/current state tracking, and no guarded transitions — it is a command-factory coordinator, not a full goal-request FSM.
rationale.D3: ArmSIM uses SingleJointedArmSim and FlywheelSIM uses DCMotorSim with physics wired into simulationPeriodic; CTRE drivetrain runs a sim thread; discounted because there is no drive-level maple-sim, no vision sim, and no deploy path/auto files indicating sim-driven development workflow.
rationale.D4: No src/test directory and no test files of any kind exist in the repo; score 0.
rationale.D5: Epilogue @Logged annotation is applied to Robot, RobotContainer, Superstructure, Arm, Flywheel, CommandSwerveDrivetrain, and LimelightSubsystem with a dual HootEpilogueBackend+NTEpilogueBackend configured in Robot.java — systematic structured logging across all subsystems; discounted from L3 because this is Epilogue (not AdvantageKit) and there are no @AutoLog inputs structs or processInputs calls.
rationale.D6: No PathPlanner or Choreo files are present; AutoRoutines uses a custom LinearPathRequest (CTRE LinearPath with PID+feedforward pose-to-pose) with hard-coded waypoint poses — this is closed-loop pose tracking beyond dead-reckoning but without authored path files or multiple competition autos, placing it between L1 and L2.
rationale.D7: LimelightSubsystem calls addVisionMeasurement with per-tag XY/theta std-dev scaled by tag count and distance-squared, rejects single-tag measurements above ambiguity threshold, and uses HootAutoReplay for timestamped replay — confirmed in LimelightSubsystem.java:135; discounted from L3 because there is no multi-camera fusion and no dedicated RobotState class with a time-interpolated buffer.
rationale.D8: Team maintains multiple repos across 2023, 2024, and 2026 seasons, published template repos (2024AdvantageKitSwerveTemplate, 2026-Template) for student onboarding, and has CI build workflows on the 2023 and 2024 season repos; discounted because the 2026 repo has no CI and there is no season-independent library package.
